body:
{
  "title": "Investor Call — Prism Ventures (2026-09-12)",
  "compiled_truth": "Initial introductory call between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann) of Prism Ventures. The meeting was scheduled after Piper reached out via a warm intro from a mutual contact at a distributed systems meetup in early September. Call lasted roughly 40 minutes, mostly spent on market framing and high-level technical positioning.\n\nSeren walked through the compression market landscape — fragmented, heavily commoditized at the consumer layer, but increasingly critical at the infrastructure layer where deterministic guarantees matter. She framed [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as occupying a gap between legacy compression tooling and the emerging need for single-pass primitives that unify compression, authentication, and transmission. Piper pushed back on market size, noting that most enterprise buyers still treat compression as a solved problem bundled into existing stacks. Seren conceded the perception issue but argued that the shift toward edge and real-time pipelines was exposing cracks — latency budgets are shrinking, and multi-pass workflows are becoming untenable for certain workloads.\n\nPiper asked about competitive moats. Seren was candid: the moat isn't a single algorithm, it's the deterministic architecture that collapses several passes into one. She described the wedge as infrastructure teams who already feel pain around data integrity in transit and are cobbling together ad hoc solutions. Piper seemed skeptical but engaged — she noted that Prism typically looks at Series A but has occasionally done large seeds when the technical differentiation is sharp enough.\n\nNo term sheet discussion. Piper asked for a follow-up deck with specifics on pipeline benchmarks and early design partners. Seren agreed to send materials by end of week. The tone was cordial but measured — Piper was clearly in diligence mode rather than conviction mode. Seren flagged that they're not actively raising yet but are building relationships ahead of a planned seed round in Q1 2027.\n\nOne notable aside: Piper mentioned that Prism had looked at two other companies in adjacent spaces earlier in the year and passed on both, which she said was partly a timing issue and partly a \"wedge clarity\" problem. She seemed to imply Anchor's framing was tighter, though she didn't commit to anything concrete. Follow-up was tentatively scheduled for late Ocotber.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-09-05** | Warm intro made between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann) via distributed systems meetup contact.",
    "- **2026-09-12** | Initial investor call held. Market overview discussion, no materials shared live.",
    "- **2026-09-16** | Seren sends follow-up deck to Piper with pipeline benchmarks and early design partner context.",
    "- **2026-09-22** | Piper acknowledges deck receipt, asks two clarifying questions about throughput guarantees.",
    "- **2026-10-28** | Follow-up call scheduled but postponed to November due to Prism's internal offsite.",
    "- **2026-11-09** | Second call with Prism Ventures — deeper technical dive with [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann) and a Prism technical advisor.",
    "- **2027-01-14** | [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) formally opens seed round; Prism Ventures receives data room access.",
    "- **2027-02-03** | Prism passes on the seed round, citing stage preference, but asks to stay in the loop for Series A."
  ]
}
